files.raw_diff:
diff --git a/app/api/routes/health.py b/app/api/routes/health.py
index a1b2c3d..e5f6g7h 100644
--- a/app/api/routes/health.py
+++ b/app/api/routes/health.py
@@ -7,12 +7,16 @@
 from __future__ import annotations
 
 import httpx
+import time
+from datetime import datetime
 
 from fastapi import APIRouter
 
 from app.core.config import get_settings
 from app.core.logging import get_logger
 from app.rag.vector_store import ChromaStore
+
+START_TIME = time.time()
 
 logger = get_logger(__name__)
 router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/health", tags=["health"])
@@ -47,11 +51,14 @@
         logger.warning("ChromaDB health check failed: %s", exc)
 
     status = "ok" if (ollama_ok and chroma_ok) else "degraded"
+    uptime_seconds = int(time.time() - START_TIME)
+    
     return {
         "status": status,
         "ollama": ollama_ok,
         "chromadb": chroma_ok,
         "llm_model": settings.OLLAMA_LLM_MODEL,
         "embed_model": settings.OLLAMA_EMBED_MODEL,
+        "uptime": f"{uptime_seconds}s",
+        "server_time": datetime.now().isoformat(),
     }

scenarios.title: Health check returns ok when all dependencies are healthy
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'ok' with all dependency flags true when both Ollama and ChromaDB are reachable and functioning correctly.
preconditions: Ollama service is running and accessible at configured OLLAMA_BASE_URL
ChromaDB is initialized and collection count operation succeeds
scenarios.expected_result: Response contains status 'ok', ollama=true, chromadb=true, and the configured llm_model name.

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded status when Ollama is unreachable
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with ollama=false when the Ollama HTTP API is not accessible or returns non-200 status.
preconditions: Ollama service is stopped or OLLAMA_BASE_URL is misconfigured
scenarios.expected_result: Response status is 'degraded', ollama=false, chromadb=true. Warning is logged for failed Ollama health check.

scenarios.title: Health check returns degraded status when ChromaDB fails initialization
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health returns status 'degraded' with chromadb=false when ChromaStore fails to initialize or count() throws an exception.
preconditions: ChromaDB is unavailable or ChromaStore() constructor throws exception
scenarios.expected_result: Response status is 'degraded', ollama=true, chromadb=false. Warning is logged for failed ChromaDB health check.

scenarios.title: Health check handles both dependencies failing simultaneously
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health correctly handles the scenario where both Ollama and ChromaDB are unavailable and returns degraded status.
preconditions: Ollama service is stopped or unreachable
scenarios.expected_result: Response status is 'degraded', ollama=false, chromadb=false. Both warnings are logged for failed dependency checks.

scenarios.title: Health check handles Ollama timeout correctly
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health handles slow Ollama responses up to the 3 second timeout and returns degraded status if timeout is exceeded.
preconditions: Ollama service responds slowly (>3 seconds) or times out
scenarios.expected_result: Response returns within reasonable time with ollama=false due to timeout exception being caught.

scenarios.title: Health endpoint is accessible without authentication
scenarios.description: Verify that /api/health endpoint is intentionally public and accessible without any authentication headers or tokens, as expected for a readiness probe.
scenarios.expected_result: Health endpoint returns 200 without requiring authentication, as expected for Kubernetes readiness probes.

scenarios.title: Health endpoint does not expose sensitive configuration data
scenarios.description: Verify that GET /api/health response only exposes safe configuration values (llm_model name) and does not leak sensitive settings like database passwords or API keys.

steps.value: Only status, ollama, chromadb, llm_model fields present

scenarios.expected_result: Response contains only non-sensitive fields: status, ollama, chromadb, llm_model. No sensitive configuration values are exposed.

scenarios.title: Health check handles unexpected HTTP methods gracefully
scenarios.description: Verify that the health endpoint properly rejects or handles non-GET HTTP methods like POST, PUT, DELETE with appropriate error responses.
scenarios.expected_result: Non-GET methods return 405 Method Not Allowed status code.

scenarios.title: Concurrent health check requests do not cause race conditions
scenarios.description: Verify that multiple simultaneous requests to GET /api/health are handled correctly without causing race conditions or resource exhaustion in ChromaStore initialization.
scenarios.expected_result: All 10 concurrent requests return 200 with consistent response structure. No exceptions are logged.

scenarios.title: Health check verifies Ollama endpoint URL is correctly constructed
scenarios.description: Verify that the health check constructs the correct Ollama API URL by appending /api/tags to the configured OLLAMA_BASE_URL.
preconditions: Ollama service running with models available
scenarios.expected_result: Health check correctly appends /api/tags path to OLLAMA_BASE_URL and successfully contacts Ollama.
